prevalence.source: European Association of Urology (EAU) context; urolithiasis epidemiology
prevalence.trend: increasing with obesity and dietary patterns

burden.dalyPerCapita: low mortality; high acute pain and recurrence burden

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Renal colic with urinalysis; ultrasound or CT; metabolic work-up for recurrent stones

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High to moderate; recurrence prevention supported by metabolic stone clinics

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Imaging (ultrasound/CT) widely available; metabolic evaluation for recurrent stones
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Hydration, analgesia, alpha-blockers; ESWL/ureteroscopy/PCNL; dietary and pharmacologic prevention
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: High recurrence and lifestyle risk factors
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Wide access to minimally invasive stone procedures and preventive programs
